attribution.text: Route, junction, and history details adapted from the Wikipedia article "List of Delaware Byways", by Wikipedia contributors, licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0.

sources.name: Federal Highway Administration — National Scenic Byways

paragraphs: The Delaware Scenic and Historic Highways program was created in 2000 by Senate Bill 320, which authorized the Delaware Department of Transportation to create a system of statewide scenic byways. In 2007, the United States Department of Transportation awarded a $174,600 grant to preserve the Route 9 and Brandywine Valley byways. The Delaware Scenic and Historic Highways program was renamed to the Delaware Byways program in 2010.

gallery.cap: Aerial View of Nemours Mansion and Gardens
gallery.desc: This 300-acre estate is comprised of the Nemours Mansion, flourishing gardens, and magnificent fountains. Location: Wilmington, Delaware Status: Public domain. Courtesy of Nemours
Blooming azaleas grace the Winterthur Estate. Location: Winterthur, Delaware Status: Public domain. Photo by A. E. Crane
gallery.credit: A. E. Crane, U.S. Department of Transportation · Public domain

gallery.desc: Many bikers enjoy the Bike and Hike activity at Hagley Museum on the Brandywine River's off-road paths. Location: Wilmington, Delaware Status: Public domain. Photo by Hagley Museum

On a warm summer's day, two people stroll along a tree-lined pedestrian path next to the Brandywine River in Brandywine River Park in Wilmington. Location: Brandywine River Park, Wilmington, Delaware Status: Public domain. Photo by A. E. Crane
gallery.credit: A. E. Crane, U.S. Department of Transportation · Public domain

gallery.cap: Brandywine Valley Byway Seen from Goodstay Mansion and Gardens
gallery.desc: The Goodstay Mansion and Gardens provide a beautiful view of the Brandywine Valley Scenic Byway along Route 52. Location: Wilmington, Delaware Status: Public domain. Photo by Gail Van Gilder

gallery.cap: Bridge and Quarry Garden at Winterthur
gallery.desc: A bridge and thriving greenery surround the Quarry Garden at the Winterthur Estate. Location: Winterthur, Delaware Status: Public domain. Photo by A. E. Crane
gallery.credit: A. E. Crane, U.S. Department of Transportation · Public domain

gallery.desc: In Brandywine River Park, people stroll over the lower pedestrian bridge while the upper stone bridge is reflected in the clear water of the Brandywine River. Location: Brandywine River Park, Wilmington, Delaware Status: Public domain. Photo by A. E. Crane
gallery.credit: A. E. Crane, U.S. Department of Transportation · Public domain

gallery.cap: Cesar Rodney Statue in Downtown Wilmington
gallery.desc: Easily found in downtown Wilmington, the Cesar Rodney Statue is considered the centerpiece of the city, and the best place to catch one of the city buses. Location: Wilmington, Delaware Status: Public domain. Photo by A. E. Crane
gallery.credit: A. E. Crane, U.S. Department of Transportation · Public domain

gallery.desc: Pictured here is a staircase decorated for the Hagley Christmas tour in this former du Pont family home located at Hagley Museum. Location: Wilmington, Delaware Status: Public domain. Photo by Hagley Museum

gallery.cap: Delaware Toy and Miniature Museum Next to Hagley Museum
gallery.desc: To the west of the entrance to Hagley Museum is the Delaware Toy and Miniature Museum. Location: Wilmington, Delaware Status: Public domain. Photo by A. E. Crane
gallery.credit: A. E. Crane, U.S. Department of Transportation · Public domain

gallery.cap: Enchanted Woods' Fairy Flower Labyrinth at Winterthur
gallery.desc: A visitor strolls the Fairy Flower Labyrinth in the Enchanted Woods garden at Winterthur. Location: Winterthur, Delaware Status: Public domain. Photo by A. E. Crane
gallery.credit: A. E. Crane, U.S. Department of Transportation · Public domain

gallery.cap: Enchanted Woods' Fairy Ring at Winterthur
gallery.desc: Winterthur's Enchanted Woods features a Fairy Ring for storytelling. Location: Winterthur, Delaware Status: Public domain. Photo by A. E. Crane
gallery.credit: A. E. Crane, U.S. Department of Transportation · Public domain

gallery.cap: Enchanted Woods' Frog Hollow at Winterthur
gallery.desc: Young girls play with the water at Frog Hollow in Winterthur's Enchanted Woods garden. Location: Winterthur, Delaware Status: Public domain. Photo by A. E. Crane
gallery.credit: A. E. Crane, U.S. Department of Transportation · Public domain

gallery.cap: Hagley Car Show at the Hagley Museum
gallery.desc: This photo shows E. Paul du Pont's Model G featured at the popular Hagley Car Show. Location: Wilmington, Delaware Status: Public domain. Photo by Hagley Museum

gallery.cap: Hagley Mill Race on the Brandywine River
gallery.desc: The Hagley Mill Race, used to generate power for the original DuPont powder mills on the Brandywine River, is located on over 230 acres preserved at the Hagley Museum and Library. Location: Wilmington, Delaware Status: Public domain. Photo by Hagley Museum

gallery.cap: Hagley Museum, the First du Pont Family Home
gallery.desc: A tour gets ready to explore the Hagley Museum, the site of the first du Pont family home and garden in America. Location: Wilmington, Delaware Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: Hedge Rows and Horse Jumps at Winterthur
gallery.desc: Permanently preserved as part of a conservation easement, Winterthur's distinct landscape features historic Osage Orange hedge rows and horse jumps used for the Point-to-Point horse racing event. Location: Winterthur, Delaware Status: Public domain. Photo by John Milner Associates
An elegant heron enjoys the trees' shade on a rock in the Brandywine River. Location: Brandywine River Park, Wilmington, Delaware Status: Public domain. Photo by A. E. Crane
gallery.credit: A. E. Crane, U.S. Department of Transportation · Public domain
